description: Test corpus for the ml-security skill. Fixtures are schema-driven code snippets; 'detect' marks code that deserializes untrusted model artifacts or hardcodes secrets in notebooks (violates a NEVER), and 'ignore' marks code using safetensors / weights_only / env-var secrets (follows ALWAYS).

fixtures.text:
import torch, requests, io

url = 'http://random-models.example.com/community/checkpoint.pt'
blob = requests.get(url).content
model = torch.load(io.BytesIO(blob))  # default weights_only=False
model.eval()
fixtures.reason: torch.load on an artifact fetched at runtime from an untrusted source with default weights_only=False executes arbitrary code via pickle. CWE-502 Deserialization of Untrusted Data; violates NEVER (torch.load an untrusted runtime artifact).

fixtures.text:
import pickle, urllib.request

raw = urllib.request.urlopen('https://hub.example.net/uploads/model.pkl').read()
clf = pickle.loads(raw)
prediction = clf.predict(features)
fixtures.reason: pickle.loads on bytes downloaded from an untrusted host runs arbitrary code on deserialization. CWE-502; violates NEVER (pickle.loads an artifact fetched at runtime from an untrusted source).

fixtures.text:
# notebook cell
import anthropic
client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key='sk-ant-api03-9fA2bQ7xR3kL8mN0pZ4tWvYuC1eHjGd')
resp = client.messages.create(model='claude-3-5-sonnet', max_tokens=100, messages=[{'role':'user','content':'hi'}])
fixtures.reason: Hard-codes an Anthropic API key directly in a notebook cell, which gets committed in plaintext. CWE-798 / violates NEVER (hard-code API keys in notebooks; use environment variables).

fixtures.text:
from safetensors.torch import load_file
from transformers import AutoModelForSequenceClassification

model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(
    'internal-registry/sentiment-v3',
    use_safetensors=True,
)
state = load_file('weights.safetensors')
model.load_state_dict(state)
fixtures.reason: Loads weights via safetensors, which is a pure-tensor format that cannot execute code on load. Follows ALWAYS (use safetensors for PyTorch/HF); safe per CWE-502.

fixtures.text:
import torch, hashlib

EXPECTED_SHA = 'a1b2c3d4e5f6...'
with open('checkpoint.pt', 'rb') as f:
    data = f.read()
assert hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest() == EXPECTED_SHA  # provenance pin
state = torch.load('checkpoint.pt', weights_only=True)
model.load_state_dict(state)
fixtures.reason: Uses weights_only=True (no arbitrary code execution) on a hash-pinned, provenance-verified local checkpoint. Follows ALWAYS (weights_only=True, pin/hash model versions); safe per CWE-502.

fixtures.text:
import os, anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'])
resp = client.messages.create(model='claude-3-5-sonnet', max_tokens=100,
                              messages=[{'role':'user','content':'hi'}])
fixtures.reason: Reads the API key from an environment variable instead of hard-coding it, so no secret is committed. Follows ALWAYS (use environment variables for secrets); safe, no CWE-798 violation.
